item.title: How Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee to replace Graham Platner
item.description: Maine Democrats are facing a ticking clock to find a new candidate to go up against GOP Sen. Susan Collins this fall, after Graham Platner dropped out of the race following a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Here's who's vying to replace Graham Platner as Maine Democratic Senate nominee
item.description: Graham Platner's move to suspend his Senate campaign after a sexual assault allegation has prompted a mad dash in Maine, as Democrats position themselves as replacements to take on GOP Sen. Susan Collins.

item.title: Iran and U.S. ramp up tit-for-tat strikes as Khamenei laid to rest
item.description: Iran's former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was buried amid an escalating exchange of fire on the 132nd day of the war.

item.title: Director of "Aces," a docuseries on the greatest male tennis players, breaks down the show
item.description: "Aces: The ATP No. 1 Club" is a new docuseries on the greatest male tennis players of all time. It features rare interviews from legends who reached number one in the world. Pat Dimon, the director of "Aces," joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security said the officer who fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston was not wearing a body camera.

item.title: Lynette Hooker may have returned to sailboat before disappearance, source says
item.description: Investigators are looking into the possibility that a Michigan woman who went missing in the Bahamas earlier this year may not have fallen overboard from a dinghy as her husband has claimed.

item.title: A Florida airport is now named after Trump, with old airport code still used for now
item.description: Palm Beach International Airport officially became Donald J. Trump International Airport on Thursday, but the airport's code won't change for another 40 days.

item.title: Breaking down the Texas app store age verification law
item.description: Earlier this week, the Supreme Court said it would allow Texas to enforce a law requiring app stores to verify users' ages while the issue plays out in the lower courts. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks down the constitutional question.

item.title: Where the U.S. is targeting strikes along the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged their heaviest strikes this week since the signing of the fragile ceasefire just over three weeks ago. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Trump's proposed arch needs structural revisions to earn final approval
item.description: Washington, D.C., has height restrictions, and the National Planning Commission offered a solution to meet them for the 250-foot proposed arch.

item.title: The food to expect from the Iowa State Fair
item.description: The Iowa State Fair opens next month, and they've just released a list of the food attendees can expect. Tony Dokoupil has the preview.

item.title: Investigators dispute Brian Hooker's account of his wife's disappearance in the Bahamas
item.description: According to Lynette Hooker's family, the story her husband Brian has been telling about the night she went missing in the Bahamas is apparently not what investigators believe actually happened. Cristian Benavides has the update.

item.title: Daughter of pastor released from Chinese prison recounts being reunited with her father
item.description: Pastor Ezra Jin has landed safely in the U.S. after being released from a Chinese prison where he spent more than 250 days for running an unregistered church. His daughter, human rights activist Grace Jin Drexel, joins The Takeout to share his story.

item.title: Democratic, GOP strategists on scramble to replace Platner, Blackburn's fortune cookie ad and more
item.description: Maine Democrats are scrambling to replace Graham Platner after he ended his U.S. Senate campaign. Political strategists Chuck Rocha and Mark Bednar join with analysis.

item.title: U.S. and Iran escalate strikes, nudging gas prices higher
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have begun the most intense fighting since President Trump struck a deal for talks three weeks ago. The U.S. hit around 90 targets in Iran overnight, and Iran responded with ballistic missiles and drones aimed at U.S. bases and military assets in the region. Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: Unstable NYC building case may make converting offices into apartments harder
item.description: The unstable midtown Manhattan building which faced the threat of collapse earlier this week may make it harder for developers to convert office spaces into apartment buildings in the future and raise insurance prices, according to the Wall Street Journal. Neil Osnato, founder of Persistence Analytics Group, joins The Takeout to discuss.

item.title: Maine Democrats navigating uncharted waters as Graham Platner says he will step down
item.description: Maine is a key state in the Democratic Party's plan to win back the U.S. Senate. But that plan is in trouble as the state's Democrats are scrambling to replace nominee Graham Platner, who is not going quietly.

item.title: Alibaba probe unearthed evidence it knowingly let dangerous drugs be sold to U.S. consumers
item.description: For eight years, Chinese online retailer Alibaba and its U.S. payment processing company failed to prevent dangerous drugs, chemicals and pill presses from being sold to American customers -- even after employees warned of compliance problems, public records show.

item.title: North Texas aviation pioneer Wally Funk dies, aged 87
item.description: Wally Funk was selected in 1961 as one of the elite women in the Mercury 13 program, finishing the same rigorous training program given to the Mercury 7 astronauts.

item.title: Charlie Kirk murder suspect said "he wishes he hadn't done it," roommate says
item.description: Lance Twiggs said in a recorded interview with a Utah prosecutor that he saw Robinson the day after Charlie Kirk was shot and killed.

item.title: Charlie Kirk shooting suspect's former roommate interview released
item.description: In the pretrial hearing of Tyler Robinson, accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, prosecutors played an interview with Tyler Robinson's former roommate and romantic partner on Thursday.

item.title: National Weather Service operating with less experienced staff, data gaps amid hurricane season
item.description: Peak hurricane season is approaching, and the National Weather Service is operating with a less experienced workforce and gaps in some data that forecasters rely on. Last year, the government agency eliminated about 15% of its workforce as part of the Trump administration's sweeping job cuts. Environmental policy expert Tracy Wholf joins "The Daily Report" with more.

item.title: A look at some candidates vying for Graham Platner's Democratic nomination for Senate
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner told his staff he will not file paperwork to officially withdraw from his race until Monday, the last day to do so. Platner said he was dropping out on Wednesday night, days after a former girlfriend accused him of sexually assaulting her in 2021. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns has more from Portland.

item.title: Affordable Care Act enrollment drops by nearly 3 million as costs rise after subsidies expire
item.description: New federal data shows that Affordable Care Act enrollments are down by nearly 3 million people. Premiums jumped at the start of 2026 after Congress let COVID-era federal subsidies expire. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins with analysis.

item.title: Trump did not use Qatar-gifted Air Force One as he left Turkey due to possible security concerns
item.description: President Trump did not use his new Qatari-gifted Air Force One when he left the NATO summit in Turkey this week due to possible security concerns, CBS News has learned. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga has more.

item.title: U.S. and Iran escalate exchange of strikes
item.description: The U.S. and Iran had significant escalations in fighting this week. The U.S. military launched more strikes on Thursday than at any other point since a fragile ceasefire was established between the two countries. CBS News senior national security correspondent Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: 7/9: CBS Evening News
item.description: The Charlie Kirk murder suspect allegedly confessed to his roommate; flash flooding sweeps the East Coast.

item.title: Home prices across the U.S. hit an all-time high
item.description: Sky-high housing prices make even starter homes too expensive for most Americans, housing data show.

item.title: 7/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Maine Democrats to hold nominating convention after Graham Platner exit; questions arise about new Air Force One plane from Qatar.

item.title: Millions of Americans have until July 10 to claim refunds from the IRS
item.description: A federal court ruling enabled some taxpayers to seek refunds tied to COVID-era filing deadlines. But that window expires July 10 — here's what to know.
